content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission


June 8, 1989




Robert E. Leidigh
Olson, Connelly, Hagel & Fong
300 Capitol Mall, Suite 350
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-89-328

Dear Mr. Leidigh:

We have received your letter of May 25, 1989 (copy attached)
seeking written confirmation of the advice I provided in our
recent telephone conversation. This is to confirm that your
letter accurately reflects that advice.

If you have any further questions, please contact me at (916)
322-5901.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

By: John G. McLean
Counsel, Legal Division

KED/JGM:aa

Attachment


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

Law Offices of

OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL & FONG

May 25, 1989


John G. McLean
Staff Counsel
Legal Division
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95814

HAND DELIVERED

RE: FOLLOW-UP CONVERSATION ON ADVICE LETTER NO. I-89-175

Dear Mr. McLean:

     This letter is to memorialize our two telephone
conversations of the past two days. These conversations
were a follow-up to my receipt of the above-referenced
advice letter. The purpose was clarification of the
advice rendered in that letter. As I discussed with you
on the telephone, if you have any disagreement with the
contents of this letter please advise me at once, as I
intend to advise my client, the Senate Rules Committee,
in accordance with this letter.

     1.     "PYI" Cards:  On this issue, I sought
clarification regarding the inclusion of such standard
phrases as "For your information" or "Per your request"
on the cards. You indicated that such phrases, including
"compliments of" would be permissible so long as they
appear below the "letterhead" logo and name of the
elected officeholder. However, it is not permissible for
them to appear above the "letterhead" logo or name of the
elected officeholder.

     Stated differently, the "letterhead" exception
assumes that the name/logotype will appear at the "head"
of the letter. Consequently, the "PYI Cards" may be
treated as being miniature letterhead stationery, subject
to the same rules to which standard-sized letterhead
stationery is subject.

     2.     Senate Committee Reports:  We discussed the
advice on this subject. You stated that the Commission's

LANCE H. OLSON
BRUCE I. HAGEL
LEROY V. FONG
ROBERT E. LEIDICH

OF COUNSEL
LLOYD G. CONNELLY, Member
California State Legislature

300 Capitol Mall, Suite 350
Sacramento, California 95814
TELEPHONE: (916) 442-2952
FAX: (916) 442-1280

John G. McLean
May 25, 1989
Page two


view is that a Senate Committee report does not qualify as an
"agency newsletter" and therefore is not eligible for the exception
allowing a roster-type listing of the committee members rather than
the standard letterhead format.

Very truly yours,

OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL & FONG

[signature]
ROBERT E. LEIDIGH

cc: Senate Rules Committee

June 1, 1989


Robert E. Leidigh
Olson, Connelly, Hagel & Fong
300 Capitol Mall, Suite 350
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Letter No. 89-328

Dear Mr. Leidigh:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act
was received on May 26, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices
Commission. If you have any questions about your advice request,
you may contact John McLean an attorney in the Legal Division,
directly at (916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions or
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. If more
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to
information needed. If your request is for informal assistance,
we will answer it as quickly as we can. (See Commission
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

[signature]

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

KED:plh


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

sections.question_synthetic: The requester sought clarification on whether standard phrases like 'For your information' or 'Per your request' and 'compliments' could appear on cards and whether Senate Committee reports qualify as 'agency newsletters' for purposes of the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Standard phrases including 'compliments' are permissible if they appear below the letterhead logo and name of the elected officeholder, but not above it. Senate Committee reports do not qualify as 'agency newsletters' and therefore are not eligible for the exception allowing roster-type listing of committee members.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains confirmation letter (June 8, 1989) referencing a prior telephone conversation and a detailed request letter (May 25, 1989) with two specific issues. Also includes an acknowledgment letter (June 1, 1989). The actual substantive advice appears in the requester's letter rather than in a formal Q/A format. Extraction confidence reduced due to fragmented text quality and lack of clearly delineated conclusion section in the FPPC response.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The requester sought clarification on whether standard phrases like 'For your information' or 'Per your request' and 'compliments' could appear on cards and whether Senate Committee reports qualify as 'agency newsletters' for purposes of the Political Reform Act.

CONCLUSION: Standard phrases including 'compliments' are permissible if they appear below the letterhead logo and name of the elected officeholder, but not above it. Senate Committee reports do not qualify as 'agency newsletters' and therefore are not eligible for the exception allowing roster-type listing of committee members.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter 89-328 confirms prior telephone advice regarding the permissibility of standard phrases on official correspondence and clarifies that Senate Committee reports do not qualify as agency newsletters under the Political Reform Act.
